methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Bright Machines
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

private.name: Carbon Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Hadrian, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company, is valued at $1.6B post-money following $560M in total capital raised. The valuation is assessed against a universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private firms operating in robotics and manufacturing at similar or adjacent stages, including Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C).

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $2.7B. Peer-median analysis across the four most closely staged comparables produces a median valuation of $1.7B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.0B. These methods reflect the heterogeneity in robotics venture valuations at this funding stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B (low) to $3.1B (high), with a median of $2.2B. The current $1.6B valuation sits at the lower quartile of this range, slightly below peer median. Key uncertainties include differentiation of Hadrian's technology versus the peer set, revenue traction relative to stage-comparable benchmarks, and the relevance of the single public comparable. The wide range between methods ($2.7B versus $1.7B) reflects meaningful dispersion in how the robotics sector is currently valued at Series C.
